name: pdx_voice
pytorch_text: This is a test of me recording my voice.
ncnn_text: This is a test of me recording my voice.
note: Best current end-to-end real voice validation sample.

name: pdx_voice_kv_cache
pytorch_text: This is a test of me recording my voice.
ncnn_text: This is a test of me recording my voice.
note: Experimental KV cache smoke test; validates text_prefill_kv + text_decode_kv on the main real voice sample.

name: kv_cache_batch_short
source: Local prepared samples from pdx-cs-sound/wavs and free-spoken-digit-dataset

results.static_text: This is a test of me recording my voice.
results.kv_text: This is a test of me recording my voice.

note: Compares default ncnn static decode against experimental ncnn KV cache decode. Original 0_jackson_0.wav and 1_jackson_0.wav are 8 kHz and were converted to 16 kHz before testing.
runtime: text128 static decode vs text48 experimental KV cache

name: hello_world
prepare_command: espeak-ng -w hello_world.wav "hello world" && ffmpeg -y -i hello_world.wav -ar 16000 -ac 1 hello_world_16k.wav

name: long_digit_five
prepare_command: for i in $(seq 1 40); do printf "file '%s'\n" "random_digit_5_jackson_0_16k.wav" >> long_digit_5_x40_concat.txt; done && ffmpeg -y -f concat -safe 0 -i long_digit_5_x40_concat.txt -c copy long_digit_5_x40_16k.wav
ncnn_text: By by by by by by five, five, five.
note: Artificial stress sample. Not a semantic correctness benchmark; tracks remaining long-audio chunk stitching limitation after overlap.

name: long_text_numbers_30
prepare_command: espeak-ng -s 360 -w long_text_numbers_30_raw.wav "one two three four five six seven eight nine ten eleven twelve thirteen fourteen fifteen sixteen seventeen eighteen nineteen twenty twenty one twenty two twenty three twenty four twenty five twenty six twenty seven twenty eight twenty nine thirty" && ffmpeg -y -i long_text_numbers_30_raw.wav -ar 16000 -ac 1 long_text_numbers_30_16k.wav
expected_text: one two three four five six seven eight nine ten eleven twelve thirteen fourteen fifteen sixteen seventeen eighteen nineteen twenty twenty one twenty two twenty three twenty four twenty five twenty six twenty seven twenty eight twenty nine thirty
ncnn_text64: One two three four five six seven eight nine ten eleven twelve thirteen 16, 17, 18, 1 twenty-four, twenty-five, twenty-six, twenty-seven, twenty
ncnn_text128: One two three four five six seven eight nine ten eleven twelve thirteen fourteen fifteen sixteen 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, 23 twenty-four, twenty-five, twenty-six, twenty-seven, twenty-eight, twenty-nine, thirty.
ncnn_text48_kv: One two three four five six seven eight nine ten eleven twelve thirteen fourteen fifteen sixteen 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, 23 twenty-four, twenty-five, twenty-six, twenty-seven, twenty-eight, twenty-nine, thirty.
note: Long-output stress sample. Static text64 truncates; static text128 and KV48 match each other under current chunk stitching baseline.
runtime: text64 fails, text128 and text48 KV align

name: long_text_numbers_fast
prepare_command: espeak-ng -s 340 -w long_text_numbers_fast.wav "one two three four five six seven eight nine ten eleven twelve thirteen fourteen" && ffmpeg -y -i long_text_numbers_fast.wav -ar 16000 -ac 1 long_text_numbers_fast_16k.wav
pytorch_text: One two three four five six seven eight nine ten eleven twelve thirteen fourteen.
ncnn_text64: One two three four five six seven eight nine ten eleven twelve thirteen
ncnn_text128: One two three four five six seven eight nine ten eleven twelve thirteen fourteen.
ncnn_text48_kv: One two three four five six seven eight nine ten eleven twelve thirteen fourteen.
note: text_seq_len=64 truncates this sample; text_seq_len=128 matches PyTorch. KV48 also emits the complete text because only prefill is bounded by text_seq_len and later tokens grow through cache.
runtime: text64 fails, text128 and text48 KV pass
